message:
feat(persistence): implement real BGSAVE via fork()+waitpid() (frankenredis-8pa9)

Replace the single-threaded BGSAVE stub — which previously called
SAVE synchronously and pretended to be asynchronous — with a proper
fork-based copy-on-write implementation matching Redis semantics:

- Track in-flight background work via two new ServerState fields:
  rdb_bgsave_pid and aof_rewrite_pid, so the main loop can detect and
  reject overlapping BGSAVE / BGREWRITEAOF requests with the exact
  error text real Redis returns ("ERR Background save already in
  progress").
- handle_bgsave_command now fork()s on unix: the child runs
  persist_snapshot_to_disk and exits with 0/1, while the parent
  records the pid and returns "Background saving started" immediately
  so clients are not blocked on disk IO.
- A new Runtime::check_child_processes() helper is invoked once per
  main-loop tick from fr-server/src/main.rs. It non-blockingly
  waitpid(WNOHANG) on both tracked pids, clears the slot when the
  child exits, and records success/failure into the store's BGSAVE
  and AOF-bgrewrite status trackers. This is what keeps
  persistence.rdb_last_bgsave_status and equivalents honest.
- Windows and other non-unix targets retain the old synchronous path
  behind cfg(not(unix)) so the build stays green on all platforms.
- The crate-level lint is softened from #![forbid(unsafe_code)] to
  #![deny(unsafe_code)] so the two fork/waitpid call sites can wear
  #[allow(unsafe_code)] attributes with a clear audit trail. Adds
  libc = "0.2.185" as a direct dependency and bumps the resolved
  version in Cargo.lock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
